title: dicranopteris lanigera
intro: Dicranopteris lanigera, also known as the forked fern or forked lady fern, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Matoniaceae and is native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Dicranopteris lanigera include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and reduce the risk of certain types of cancer. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it can also be used topically to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne.
